violations.description: Conditional test skip based on runtime element visibility check
violations.suggestion: Seed the database with required test data using factories before running the test, or mock the response so that the element is guaranteed to be visible.

violations.description: Conditional test skip based on seeded data availability check
violations.suggestion: Use API or direct database seeding in a beforeEach hook to ensure at least one report exists, removing the need for runtime conditional skipping.

violations.description: Conditional test skip based on seeded client data availability check
violations.suggestion: Seed the test client dynamically inside the test suite setup to guarantee it exists rather than checking and skipping at runtime.

recommendations: Ensure all tests actively provision their own required data via API/seeding rather than relying on global database state.
Remove runtime conditional blocks and test.skip() statements to ensure tests either run fully or fail explicitly.
summary: The test suite has no high severity determinism issues like Math.random() or hardcoded date/time objects. However, there are 3 medium-severity violations due to conditional test skipping (test.skip()) depending on seeded data visibility, which reduces determinism and can hide integration/seeding failures.
